content.text: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP through oxidative phosphorylation. They have a double membrane — the inner membrane is highly folded into cristae, maximizing surface area for the electron transport chain. Fun fact: they have their own DNA, supporting the endosymbiotic theory.

points: Prophase: chromosomes condense, spindle fibers begin to form
Metaphase: chromosomes align at the cell's equator (metaphase plate)
Anaphase: sister chromatids separate and move to opposite poles
Telophase: nuclear envelopes reform, chromosomes decondense
Cytokinesis: cytoplasm divides to produce two daughter cells

subject: History
title: The Fall of the Berlin Wall

content.text: On November 9, 1989, the Berlin Wall fell after 28 years of dividing East and West Berlin. Growing pressure from peaceful protests and political reform in Eastern Europe led the East German government to open the border. It became the symbol of the end of the Cold War and led to German reunification in 1990.

content.question: What is the quadratic formula used to solve ax² + bx + c = 0?
content.answer: x = (-b ± √(b² - 4ac)) / 2a. The discriminant (b² - 4ac) determines the number of solutions: positive = 2 real, zero = 1 real, negative = 2 complex.

content.question: What is the time complexity of binary search on a sorted array of n elements?

content.text: DNA replication is semi-conservative — each new double helix contains one original and one new strand. Helicase unwinds the helix, primase adds RNA primers, and DNA polymerase III synthesizes the new strand in the 5'→3' direction. The leading strand is continuous; the lagging strand is built in Okazaki fragments.

points: Militarism: European nations built up massive armies and navies
Alliances: complex web of treaties meant one conflict could drag in many nations
Imperialism: competition for colonies created international tensions
Nationalism: ethnic pride fueled rivalries, especially in the Balkans
Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand was the immediate trigger

content.text: In any right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of the squares of the other two sides: a² + b² = c². This is one of the most fundamental relationships in geometry, used in distance calculations, trigonometry, and even 3D space.

points: 301 Moved Permanently — resource has a new URL
404 Not Found — server can't find the resource
500 Internal Server Error — server encountered an unexpected condition

content.question: Which organelle is responsible for protein synthesis?
When and where did the Renaissance begin, and what was its core idea?
content.answer: The Renaissance began in 14th-century Italy (Florence). Its core idea was humanism — a renewed focus on human potential, classical learning, art, and science, marking the transition from the medieval to the modern world.

content.question: What is the derivative of f(x) = x³ + 2x?

content.text: An API (Application Programming Interface) is a set of rules that lets different software applications communicate. Think of it as a waiter in a restaurant: you (the client) tell the waiter (API) what you want, and they bring it from the kitchen (server). REST APIs use HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, DELETE.

content.question: What is the overall chemical equation for photosynthesis?
content.answer: 6CO₂ + 6H₂O + light energy → C₆H₁₂O₆ + 6O₂. Carbon dioxide and water are converted into glucose and oxygen using sunlight, primarily in the chloroplasts.

content.text: The French Revolution (1789–1799) overthrew the monarchy and established France as a republic. Triggered by economic crisis, social inequality, and Enlightenment ideas, key events included the storming of the Bastille, the Declaration of the Rights of Man, and the Reign of Terror. It fundamentally changed European political systems.
